title: planchonella microphylla
intro: Planchonella microphylla, also known as the mountain apple or bush apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. This plant is native to the tropical forests of New Guinea and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Planchonella microphylla include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Planchonella microphylla can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
